curatedFacts.founded: 1834, originally named York (1793), renamed Toronto in 1834
curatedFacts.landmarks: CN Tower, Royal Ontario Museum, Distillery District, Toronto Islands, Eaton Centre, Kensington Market, St. Lawrence Market, Hockey Hall of Fame
curatedFacts.notable: Canada's largest city and the most multicultural city in the world — over 200 distinct ethnic groups, 140+ languages spoken, and half the population born outside Canada
curatedFacts.economy: financial capital of Canada, Bay Street, Toronto Stock Exchange, tech hub, film industry ('Hollywood North'), healthcare, education (U of T, Ryerson, York)
curatedFacts.entityDesc: Toronto is the largest city in Ontario and a city in Greater Toronto Area, Ontario, Canada, with a population of approximately 2,794,356. Financial capital of canada, bay street, toronto stock exchange, tech hub, film industry ('hollywood north'), healthcare, education (u of t, ryerson, york).
curatedFacts.longDesc:
Toronto isn't just Ontario's city — it's a place where ambition meets opportunity. Founded originally named York (1793), renamed Toronto in 1834, this city has transformed into a world-class metropolis of over ~2.9 million (city), ~6.2 million (GTA metro) people.

What makes Toronto remarkable for {BUSINESS_TYPE}? Scale and diversity. The city encompasses everything from world-class dining, vibrant arts scene, 1,600+ parks, the PATH underground walkway (30km), and professional sports teams (Maple Leafs, Raptors, Blue Jays, TFC). Each neighborhood has its own character, its own needs, and its own expectations.

The local economy is driven by financial capital of Canada, Bay Street, Toronto Stock Exchange, tech hub, film industry ('Hollywood North'), healthcare, education (U of T, Ryerson, York). This economic foundation creates steady demand for services and supports a community that takes pride in maintaining its properties and infrastructure.

Community features include CN Tower, Royal Ontario Museum, Distillery District, Toronto Islands, Eaton Centre, Kensington Market, St. Lawrence Market, Hockey Hall of Fame, which give Toronto its distinctive character.

Toronto benefits from its location in Greater Toronto Area. Whether you need routine {SERVICE} maintenance or a major project, {COMPANY_NAME} understands what Toronto properties need. We've invested the time to learn this community — the local building codes, the weather patterns, the neighbourhood expectations. That's why Toronto residents keep choosing {COMPANY_NAME}.
